# Groundwater Geochemistry

**Summary**: The study of the chemical composition and evolution of groundwater in aquifer systems.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] focused on groundwater-geochemistry to evaluate water quality and mineral interaction.

## Key Subtopics

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] ['mineral dissolution', 'seawater intrusion', 'cation exchange']

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] ['hydrochemical-analysis', 'equilibrium modeling']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Groundwater follows Chebotarev's sequence, with local variations due to seawater intrusion and cation exchange. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 19]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] Identifying impacts of seawater intrusion and cation exchange.
